message:
fix: leaderboard push retry defeated by bash set -e (#1542)

The retry loop added in #1540 used '&& break' which causes bash
set -e (GitHub Actions default) to exit the script on the first
push failure, before retries can run. Wrap the push in an if-then
block so failures are caught by the conditional instead of
triggering set -e exit.

Also increase retries to 5 with a 3s delay, and exit 1 explicitly
after exhausting all attempts.

Fixes #1541

Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -53,12 +53,18 @@ jobs:
           git add public/data/leaderboard.json public/data/contributors/
           git diff --cached --quiet && echo "No changes" && exit 0
           git commit -m "chore: update leaderboard and contributor profile data"
-          MAX_RETRIES=3
+          MAX_RETRIES=5
+          RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS=3
           for i in $(seq 1 $MAX_RETRIES); do
-            git pull --rebase origin main && git push && break
-            echo "Push attempt $i failed, retrying..."
-            sleep 2
+            if git pull --rebase origin main && git push; then
+              echo "Push succeeded on attempt $i"
+              exit 0
+            fi
+            echo "Push attempt $i/$MAX_RETRIES failed, retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS}s..."
+            sleep "$RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS"
           done
+          echo "All $MAX_RETRIES push attempts failed"
+          exit 1
 
       - name: Create issue on failure
         if: failure()
